task_summary: Build and benchmark ISCI on Marson CD4+ Perturb-seq, CPU-local, with a leak-controlled ablation figure and CAR-T clinical bridge

phases.name: D0 — Data, axes, and the leak-controlled core

steps.title: Load data + provenance manifest
steps.description: Implement isci/io.py: load GWCD4i.DE_stats.h5ad and pseudobulk_merged.h5ad from data/ (download via aws s3 --no-sign-request), SHA-256 every input, write an execution manifest (versions, hashes, seeds, git-SHA). Confirm .obs/.layers/.varm structure matches method.md §12a.

steps.title: Build axes with leave-one-out
steps.description: Implement isci/axes.py: build unit-normalized signature vectors u_a from config/axes.yaml curated markers + data-native Th1/Th2 and activation loadings. Add leave-one-out mode that rebuilds every axis with a scored gene removed (fixes C1). Cross-check markers against CELLxGENE via mcp-cellguide.

steps.title: Compute M and R
steps.description: Implement isci/movement.py (M = percentile-ranked |cosine(z, u_a)| with sign kept) and isci/repro.py (R from donor_correlation_hits_mean, guide_correlation_signif, gated by ontarget_significant). Compute per (g,a,c).

steps.title: Baselines + ground-truth benchmark
steps.description: Implement isci/baselines.py (DE-magnitude, effect-size, centrality-only) and isci/validate.py ground_truth_recovery (AUROC/AUPRC/precision@k). Assemble positive set (clinical-curated leads; Marson-native secondary) and expression-matched negatives via GTEx (mcp-expression). Report all metrics under leave-one-out.

steps.title: D0 ISCI = R x geomean(M) + report
steps.description: Implement isci/index.py minimal aggregation (R-gated M) with epsilon floor. Produce the first ranked gene table and a draft AUROC readout. This is the guaranteed minimum submission.

phases.name: D1–D2 — Network control, stability, ablation figure

steps.title: Connector-grounded GRN
steps.description: Implement isci/network.py: build a directed GRN from CollecTRI priors intersected with STRING (mcp-protein-annotation) and JASPAR/UniBind TFBS (mcp-regulation) edges, restricted to measured genes. Avoid fragile de-novo GRNBoost2.

steps.title: Influence-based D score
steps.description: Score D as a continuous influence/controllability contribution (fraction of minimal driver sets containing g, or CEFCON-style influence), never binary MFVS/MDS membership (fixes C5). Percentile-rank to [0,1].

steps.title: Magnitude-residualized stability S
steps.description: Implement isci/stability.py using shesha-geometry: compute geometric coherence, then residualize against effect magnitude so S is orthogonal to M (fixes C3). Use by_guide/by_donor replicate dispersion as the pseudobulk proxy and validate against true single-cell Shesha on one subsampled perturbation set.

steps.title: A via pert2state linear concordance
steps.description: Implement isci/insilico.py: run the pert2state linear model (also the mandated must-beat baseline) and compute A as predicted-vs-observed axis-projection concordance. No GEARS/CellOracle (CPU-local decision).

steps.title: Full aggregation + component-appropriate nulls
steps.description: Complete isci/index.py: ISCI = R x S x geomean(M,D,A) with epsilon floor; null via label permutation for M, degree-preserving rewiring for D, within-perturbation guide shuffling for S (fixes C6); BH-FDR q-values.

steps.title: Central ablation figure
steps.description: Implement isci/validate.py ablation_curve; render the central figure (AUROC/AUPRC for ISCI-full vs -D/-A/-S vs M+R-only vs each baseline incl. pert2state) using figure-style. Include cross-donor and cross-condition holdouts.

phases.name: D3–D4 — External transfer and clinical bridge

steps.title: Frangieh transfer
steps.description: Load Frangieh Perturb-CITE-seq via pertpy (pt.data.frangieh_2021); test rank-correlation of ISCI controllability across datasets using pertpy Distance/Augur. Do this first (zero download friction).

steps.title: Belk + Schmidt transfer
steps.description: Pull Belk 2022 and Schmidt 2022 via mcp-omics-archives (GEO); test whether Marson-trained ISCI recovers their hits better than raw DE. Belk is the ARID1A/INO80 Gladstone tie-in.

steps.title: Project ISCI onto CAR-T atlas
steps.description: Load the Functional CAR-T atlas (Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.17213452) using its pre-computed scVI-hub latent (no re-integration). Build a per-sample ISCI controllability signature score and project it onto the atlas.

steps.title: Phenotype separation floor
steps.description: Before any outcome test, verify the ISCI controllability signature separates exhaustion vs memory/stem phenotypes in the atlas latent (near-guaranteed positive control). This is the minimum-acceptable D4 result: a figure that lands even if outcome separation is weak. Correct for study/batch (13 studies) before interpreting.

steps.title: Responder vs non-responder test
steps.description: Implement isci/validate.py project_clinical_signature: only after the phenotype floor lands, test responder/non-responder separation (AUROC) with study as a covariate/holdout, benchmark against a TCF7/FOXO1-regulon reference. Report honestly if underpowered; keep hypothesis-generated strictly separate from evidence-supported.

steps.title: Verify CPU feasibility of borzoi/evo2
steps.description: Before promising anything: load the borzoi and evo2 skills and confirm a handful of loci can be scored on CPU-local within demo time. If infeasible CPU-local, stop here and report — do not reopen remote compute unless the user asks.

steps.title: Regulatory-variant effect on top controllers
steps.description: For ~10-20 top ISCI controllers (e.g. FOXO1, TOX, ARID1A), score predicted chromatin/expression effects of regulatory variants in their loci using borzoi/evo2 + mcp-regulation (ENCODE ATAC) and mcp-genomes (VEP/UCSC). One focused mechanistic-validation figure. Runs only if D2+D4 close early; first to cut under time pressure.

phases.name: Continuous — Evidence cards, report, demo, packaging

steps.title: Per-gene evidence cards
steps.description: Implement isci/evidence.py: for each top-ranked gene, build a claim->citation card from PubMed (mcp-pubmed) + Open Targets (mcp-open-targets) + literature-review skill, fact-checked, no hallucinated references. Fan out with sub-agents for parallelism (Claude Use).

steps.title: Clinician report
steps.description: Draft a clinician-readable report tying top controllers to CAR-T failure / bispecific resistance, using hematology-medical-writer. Flag explicitly where resistance is tumor-intrinsic or microenvironmental (out of ISCI scope).

steps.title: Demo, summary, MIT repo, skill
steps.description: Assemble the 3-minute demo assets (problem -> ISCI figure -> live evidence card -> ablation -> clinical bridge), the 100-200 word written summary, ensure MIT license and event-timestamped commit history, and package the pipeline as a reusable Claude Science skill with per-artifact provenance.

desired_outputs: Ranked ISCI gene table (per-axis + overall) with null-permutation q-values, saved as CSV
Central ablation figure: AUROC/AUPRC for ISCI-full vs ablated variants vs baselines (DE-magnitude, effect-size, centrality, pert2state), under leave-one-out axis construction
Per-gene evidence cards for top controllers (claim -> PubMed/Open Targets citation, fact-checked) as markdown/JSON
Clinical-bridge figure: ISCI controllability signature projected onto Functional CAR-T atlas latent, responder vs non-responder separation (AUROC)
Reproducibility manifest (data SHA-256, env, seeds, git-SHA) welded to every artifact
Implemented isci/ Python package (io, axes, movement, repro, network, insilico, stability, index, baselines, validate, evidence)
3-minute demo assets + 100-200 word written summary + MIT repo, packaged as a reusable Claude Science skill

feasibility.rationale: Core index, benchmark, and ablation (D0-D2) are firmly feasible CPU-local on DE_stats/pseudobulk; leakage and identifiability fixes are well-scoped. The clinical bridge (D4) is de-risked with a guaranteed phenotype-separation floor before the outcome test, which may still be underpowered. The Gladstone chromatin layer (D5) is an explicit cut-first stretch, gated on CPU feasibility. S's independent value depends on magnitude-residualization holding up empirically.
